text:
Women, Infants and Children (WIC) Program | Sutter County, CA
Show/Hide
ROAD CLOSURES
COMMUNITY ACADEMY
What is WIC?
The Women, Infants & Children (WIC) Program is a federally-funded health and nutrition program.
Who can WIC help?
The WIC program is for all kinds of families: married and single parents, working or not working. If you are a father, mother, grandparent, foster parent or other legal guardian of a child under the age of five and fall under any of the categories below, you can apply for WIC.
Pregnant woman (starting at the first trimester)
New mom
Breastfeeding and bottle-feeding moms
Children under 5 years of age
Infant
Migrant families
Military families
How can WIC help me?
At no cost, WIC provides you and your family with education, food, and resources.
Nutrition Education
Nutrition and health assessments
Educational material and cookbooks to get you and your family eating healthy
Breastfeeding Support
Lactation support
Breast pump loans
Breastfeeding peer counseling support
Groceries
Offers healthy foods such as:
Fruits & vegetables
Whole grains
Baby food
Milk and much more!
Community Resources
Referrals to doctors, dentists, and other community agencies and programs
How to Apply
